name: profile-scope test uses fragile double-invocation try/catch for status
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L4-09] function invoked twice (toThrow then try/catch) to check .status; brittle if throw type changes. Fix: expect(()=>...).toThrow(expect.objectContaining({status:400})).
acceptanceCriteria:
Root cause: the profile-scope negative tests invoke the helper once for toThrow and a second time inside try/catch to inspect status, so assertion behavior is brittle if the throw type, status, or side effects change.
Variants:
- requireProfileId(undefined) must throw an HTTPException with status 400.
- requireAccount(undefined) must throw an HTTPException with status 401 and the account-required message.
Regression: update apps/api/src/middleware/profile-scope.test.ts so each negative-path test captures/asserts the thrown HTTPException status from a single invocation and fails if the wrong status/type is thrown; existing success-path tests stay green.
